macro-F1: An accuracy score that averages performance on both answer types equally, so you can't win by only getting the common one right.
binary-F1: An accuracy score focused on one target class only — here, catching hallucinated answers.
all-0 probe: A test submission that labels every row hallucinated, used to reverse-engineer the hidden scoring formula.
base rate: The fraction of examples in a class — here, how often answers are hallucinated.
intrinsic: A response that must be checked against a given source passage.
extrinsic: A response with no source passage — must be checked against real-world knowledge.
NLI: Natural Language Inference — a model that judges whether one text is supported by another.
closed-book: Answering from memory with no reference passage provided.
Phase-2: The offline round where organizers re-run your code on hidden data; worth 50% of the final score.
C1 band: The most culturally-specific Bangladesh-knowledge slice, used as the tie-breaker.
